content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers, initially all zero.

There are QQQ types of operations that you are allowed to perform. Each operation is described by two integers lil_ili‚Äč and rir_iri‚Äč (1‚Č§li‚Č§ri‚Č§N)(1 \leq l_i \leq r_i \leq N)(1‚Č§li‚Äč‚Č§ri‚Äč‚Č§N) and is as follows:


Set Aj=Aj‚äē1A_j = A_j \oplus 1Aj‚Äč=Aj‚Äč‚äē1 for each jjj such that li‚Č§j‚Č§ril_i \leq j \leq r_ili‚Äč‚Č§j‚Č§ri‚Äč.
Here ‚äē\oplus‚äē represents the Bitwise XOR operator.

Your task is to find the number of distinct arrays which can be obtained by applying some subset of these operations (possibly none, possibly all). Since the answer may be huge, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line contains two integers NNN and QQQ ‚ÄĒ the size of the array AAA and the number of types of operations you can perform.
The iii-th of the next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers lil_ili‚Äč and rir_iri‚Äč ‚ÄĒ corresponding to the iii-th query.


Output Format
Output in single line, the number of distinct arrays which can be obtained by applying some subset of these operations, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
